'Fundamental blessings' frames the inheritance as both practically important and morally valuable

cmt.annotation_notes: ext_015 member: this sentence (legal inheritors) and p02_s04 (legacy bequeathed by departed ancestors) form the inheritance-claim arc in p02. inst_00078 establishes the inheritance status (we are the heirs); inst_00079 specifies the bequeathing act (the legacy given by the departed ancestors). The two together constitute the foundational cluster_05 claim of the Lyceum Address. Moved legacy sacrificial_economy prose into sacrificial_yield. koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The present generation found itself in legal possession of an inheritance it did not earn — political institutions bequeathed by the founding generation. instances.span_text: a legacy bequeathed us, by a _once_ hardy, brave, and patriotic, but _now_ lamented and departed race of ancestors

cmt.source_domain: Testamentary legacy — ancestors bequeathing their achievements as a legacy to descendants; the founding generation as a departed testator
cmt.target_domain: The political institutions and freedoms as a legacy bequeathed by the founding generation who are now dead — their achievements constitute the present generation's patrimony
entailments: 'Bequeathed' is testamentary language — the founding generation formally left us their achievements as an inheritance
The once/now contrast (hardy, brave, patriotic vs. lamented and departed) frames the founding generation as a race that has ceased to exist — their deaths make the legacy both precious and fragile
We toiled not — the present generation received the inheritance without effort; this creates the obligation to preserve what we did not earn
The 'departed race' formula frames the founding generation as a distinct people, not just historical predecessors — their extinction is a loss that creates urgency
cmt.annotation_notes: ext_015 member paired with inst_00078. This is the most explicit cluster_05 instance in the Lyceum Address — the foundational patrimony claim stated in testamentary language. 'Legacy bequeathed' is the inheritance frame's most precise legal formulation. The 'once...now' structure (hardy, brave, patriotic → lamented and departed) performs the elegiac function that will recur in cluster_05 throughout the corpus: the founders were great; they are gone; we must prove worthy of what they left us. This is cluster_05's earliest full deployment in the corpus. Moved legacy sacrificial_economy prose into sacrificial_yield. Migrated legacy fantasy_type 'founding_authority_as_rule' to canonical 'ancestral_debt'. Migrated legacy absence_flag 'disease_and_purification' to canonical 'disease_purification_absent'.

mapping.a legacy bequeathed us: the founding generation's achievements as a testamentary bequest — bequeathed (legal term for inheritance transfer) frames it as a formal inheritance act
mapping.a _once_ hardy, brave, and patriotic...r: the founding generation as a distinct people, now dead — their heroic qualities make the legacy precious
mapping._now_ lamented and departed: the founding generation as departed testators — they are dead; we are their inheritors. 'Lamented' frames their departure as a loss

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The founding generation bequeathed the political institutions as a legacy — and they are now dead. The present generation toiled not in the acquirement; they received it freely. This creates an obligation of stewardship: we must preserve what was given to us by a race of ancestors who earned it through hardship. The departed founders' death makes the inheritance both precious and obligating — if it is lost, there is no second chance to receive it.
koenigsberg.projected_entity: The founding generation as departed testators whose legacy — political institutions — constitutes the present generation's patrimony
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution: Those who fail to preserve the bequest violate the obligation of grateful stewardship — they dishonor the legacy of the 'lamented and departed race'

instances.span_text: live through all time, or die by suicide

cmt.source_domain: Organism life and death — a nation as a living being that can live indefinitely or kill itself through self-inflicted destruction
cmt.target_domain: The American republic as a living organism whose only mortal danger is internal self-destruction (mob violence, disregard for law), not external attack
entailments: The republic's only mortal threat is internal — foreign armies cannot kill it; only Americans can
'Suicide' frames internal self-destruction as a freely chosen death — the national organism kills itself

This is the earliest nation-as-organism instance in the corpus — 1838, 20 years before the House Divided Speech
cmt.annotation_notes: This is the earliest cluster_01 instance in the corpus (1838). The organism frame here is existential: the national body has infinite lifespan unless it kills itself. The 'suicide' variant is the most internally focused form of cluster_01 — the threat is not wound, disease, or external attack but self-inflicted death. Contrast with the House Divided Speech (1858) where the architectural/structural frame makes the threat division-as-collapse rather than self-murder. The Lyceum Address's organism-suicide is a distinct early variant of cluster_01: life/death rather than stand/fall. Moved legacy sacrificial_economy prose into sacrificial_yield. Migrated legacy fantasy_type 'national_integrity_threat' to canonical 'wound_and_healing'. Migrated legacy absence_flag 'disease_and_purification' to canonical 'disease_purification_absent'.

mapping.live through all time: the nation as organism with indefinite lifespan — it need not die; it can persist eternally

mapping.As a nation of freemen: the qualifier that gives the organism its unique property — because it is free, it cannot be destroyed by external force

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The republic as a living organism can only die by its own hand. External threats — foreign armies, tyrants — cannot destroy it. Only internal breakdown (mob violence, disregard for law, attachment to an ambitious tyrant) can. The suicide frame makes the preservation of the republic's life a matter of internal discipline rather than external defense. The organism must be protected from itself.
koenigsberg.projected_entity: The American republic as a living organism uniquely immune to external destruction but vulnerable to internal self-murder
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution: Those who contribute to mob violence, disregard for law, or attachment to a Caesar-figure are participating in national suicide

instances.span_text: trample on the blood of his father

cmt.source_domain: Blood inheritance — the founding generation's sacrifice as a blood-covenant; violating the law is desecrating the blood of the fathers who fought for it
cmt.target_domain: The obligation to revere the laws as inherited from the founding generation whose blood paid for them — law-violation is blood-desecration
entailments: The laws are blood-consecrated — established by the founders' sacrifice, the laws partake of their blood
Violating the law is an act of literal desecration — trampling on something sacred
The blood-inheritance frame makes law-reverence not just a civic duty but a moral obligation derived from the founding sacrifice
This is cluster_05's most visceral form — not legal inheritance or testamentary legacy but blood-covenant inheritance
cmt.annotation_notes: This is the most novel cluster_05 instance in the Lyceum Address — the blood-covenant inheritance variant. 'Trample on the blood of his father' is marked novel because the blood-as-inheritance-medium is not a conventional inheritance metaphor. The preceding sentence (p12_s02) has each American 'swear by the blood of the Revolution' — so the oath is sworn on blood, and violating it is trampling on blood. This blood-covenant structure will recur in different forms throughout the corpus (Gettysburg's honored dead, Second Inaugural's blood drawn by the sword) but here appears in its earliest and most direct form: the founding sacrifice as the consecrating material of the political covenant. Migrated legacy absence_flag 'disease_and_purification' to canonical 'disease_purification_absent'.

mapping.trample on the blood of his father: violating the law = desecrating the blood the founding generation shed to establish it. Blood is the inheritance medium — the founding sacrifice.
mapping.the blood of his father: the founding generation's sacrifice literalized as blood — the material cost of establishing the inherited institutions
mapping.swear by the blood of the Revolution: the oath sworn on the founders' blood (p12_s02 preceding context) — the inheritance is blood-consecrated

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The laws are consecrated by the blood the founding generation shed to establish them. To violate the law is to trample on the blood of the father — an act of desecration and ingratitude. This is cluster_05's blood-covenant variant: the inheritance is not testamentary (legacy bequeathed) or juridical (precedent decided) but sacrificial. The founders' blood becomes the sacred material that consecrates the political institutions, and the obligation to preserve them becomes a duty not to desecrate the blood of the fathers.
koenigsberg.projected_entity: The founding generation as blood-sacrificers whose shed blood consecrates the political institutions
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution: Those who violate the law trample on the fathers' blood — they desecrate the sacred sacrifice of the founding generation
koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: The founding generation's blood is the original sacrifice that establishes the covenant. The present generation's obligation is to honor that sacrifice by law-reverence. This is the earliest sacrificial economy in the corpus — but it is retrospective (the founding sacrifice already paid) rather than prospective (new sacrifices required).

instances.span_text: an undecided experiment; now, it is understood to be a successful one

cmt.source_domain: Scientific experiment — a test whose outcome was uncertain but has now been determined by results
cmt.target_domain: The American republic as an experiment in self-governance that was once undecided but has now been proved successful by historical demonstration
entailments: The founding generation was running the experiment — they were the scientists who designed and executed the test
The 'undecided' status was genuine uncertainty — the outcome was not predetermined but depended on whether the republic could sustain itself
The experiment is now complete — 'successful' in the past tense means the proof has been made
This creates the subsequent problem: if the experiment is done, what motivates the next generation to maintain what was already proved?
cmt.annotation_notes: ext_014 member: the first of three cluster_03 instances in p17. This sentence presents the temporal arc: the republic was once undecided (experiment uncertain) and is now understood as successful. The arc from undecided to successful is the core of the cluster_03 logic — the republic as proof. Contrast with Gettysburg's 'testing whether' formula (1863), which reopens the experimental uncertainty that the Lyceum Address declares closed in 1838. The Civil War will make the experiment undecided again. Moved legacy sacrificial_economy prose into sacrificial_yield. Migrated legacy fantasy_type 'republic_as_proposition_under_test' to canonical 'experiment_and_proof'. Migrated legacy absence_flag 'disease_and_purification' to canonical 'disease_purification_absent'.

mapping.an undecided experiment: the republic during the founding period as a test whose outcome was uncertain — could it work? Was it possible?
mapping.now, it is understood to be a successful: the present assessment — the experiment has been run, the results are in, the republic has been proved viable

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The republic was an undecided experiment that has now been proved successful. The founding generation's task was to run the experiment; they succeeded. But with success comes a new problem: the proof is complete, and new ambitious men will not find satisfaction in maintaining what others proved. The experiment frame generates an obligation to preserve the proof — not to let a successfully completed experiment be destroyed by internal ambition or mob violence.
koenigsberg.projected_entity: The American republic as a completed scientific experiment whose positive result must be preserved
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution: Those who destroy the republic undermine a successful proof — they negate the experiment's result

cmt.source_domain: Logical proof / scientific demonstration — proving the truth of a proposition by practical demonstration rather than theoretical argument
cmt.target_domain: The American republic as a practical demonstration of the proposition that self-governance is possible — a truth that had been theoretically doubted and required practical proof
entailments: The founding generation did not argue for self-governance's possibility — they demonstrated it
The republic is itself the proof: its existence and success is the demonstration
'Problematical' means genuinely uncertain before demonstration — the experiment was not predetermined to succeed

cmt.annotation_notes: ext_014 member: the fullest articulation of cluster_03 in the Lyceum Address. Where p17_s06 presents the experiment/success summary, this sentence presents the logical-proposition variant: the republic is a 'practical demonstration of the truth of a proposition.' The founding generation were not builders or warriors but demonstrators of a logical proof. This is cluster_03's most explicit early formulation in the corpus — more explicit than Gettysburg's compressed 'testing whether' because Lincoln in 1838 states the proposition directly ('the capability of a people to govern themselves') and names the prior epistemic status ('problematical'). The Gettysburg version will compress this into a four-word subordinate clause. Moved legacy sacrificial_economy prose into sacrificial_yield. Migrated legacy absence_flag 'disease_and_purification' to canonical 'disease_purification_absent'.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The founding generation's ambition was to demonstrate the truth of a proposition — that self-governance is possible. The problem the Lyceum Address now confronts is: what happens after the proof is complete? The ambitious men of the next generation cannot find satisfaction in maintaining a completed proof. They must seek new experiments — and those new experiments are dangerous to the republic.

koenigsberg.guilt_distribution: Those who seek to overturn the republic for personal ambition are undermining a completed and successful experiment
koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: Thousands won deathless names in making the experiment successful — their sacrifice is acknowledged but not required again. The founding sacrifice is complete.

instances.span_text: the pillars of the temple of liberty; and now, that they have crumbled away, that temple must fall, unless we, their descendants, supply their places with other pillars

cmt.source_domain: Architectural inheritance — the founding generation as the structural pillars of a temple; their death as the crumbling of those pillars; the present generation's obligation to supply replacement pillars hewn from reason
cmt.target_domain: The obligation of the present generation to replace the founding generation's function as structural supports of the republic — the fathers were the pillars; we must be their replacements
entailments: The founding generation's function was structural, not just inspirational — they were pillars that actually held up the temple
Their deaths create a structural deficiency — the temple cannot stand on crumbled pillars
The inheritance obligation is architectural: we must supply new pillars, not just preserve the old ones
The shift from passion-based pillars (the founders' revolutionary fervor) to reason-based pillars (sober reason) is the Lyceum Address's political argument in compressed form
cmt.annotation_notes: The most architecturally explicit cluster_05 instance in the corpus. The 'pillars of the temple of liberty' frame is novel — treating the founding generation not as ancestors or legal testators but as architectural structural elements. This creates an unusual inheritance obligation: we must not preserve the pillars (they have crumbled) but replace them with new ones. The material specification ('hewn from the solid quarry of sober reason') anticipates the Lyceum Address's concluding argument: the next generation must substitute reason for passion as the basis of civic attachment. The founding generation's passion-based patriotism cannot be replicated; reason-based patriotism must take its place. Moved legacy sacrificial_economy prose into sacrificial_yield. Migrated legacy fantasy_type 'founding_authority_as_rule' to canonical 'ancestral_debt'. Migrated legacy absence_flag 'disease_and_purification' to canonical 'disease_purification_absent'.

mapping.unless we, their descendants, supply the: the inheritance obligation: the present generation must replace the founding pillars with new ones
mapping.hewn from the solid quarry of sober reas: the material for the replacement pillars — reason rather than passion. The source of the new structural supports

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The founding generation were the pillars of the temple of liberty. They have crumbled. The temple must fall unless we — their descendants — supply replacement pillars. This is cluster_05's architectural-inheritance variant: the obligation is not to receive a legacy (legal inheritance) or honor a blood-covenant (blood of the father) but to actively replace structural elements that have been lost. The inheritance is not property to be stewarded but a structural role to be filled.
koenigsberg.projected_entity: The founding generation as the structural pillars of the republic — architectural supports whose loss threatens collapse
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution: Those who fail to supply replacement pillars allow the temple to fall — they are responsible for the collapse through inaction

koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The founders are idealized as fathers; their actual compromises on slavery — including the three-fifths clause and the 20-year extension of the slave trade — are structurally absent from 'our fathers brought forth a new nation... dedicated to the proposition that all men are created equal.'
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Enslaved people are absent from the founding paternal gift. The proposition 'all men are created equal' was not applied to them at founding; the paternal lineage metaphor renders them non-heirs of the founding promise even as the address will claim to be fulfilling that promise.

instances.span_text: brought forth on this continent, a new nation, conceived in Liberty

cmt.source_domain: birth_generation
cmt.target_domain: national_founding
entailments: the_founding_is_a_birth_act
founders_are_parents
the_nation_is_a_child
liberty_is_the_medium_of_conception
birth_requires_labor
a_born_entity_can_die
cmt.annotation_notes: The double birth metaphor — 'brought forth' (obstetric) and 'conceived' (gestational) — is deliberate and dense. 'Conceived in Liberty' fuses the ideological (conceived in the idea of liberty) with the biological (literally conceived). This entails that the nation is a living organism capable of dying, which the rest of the address will require.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: No obligatory frame here; this sentence establishes the fact of birth, not obligation.
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The founding-as-birth metaphor idealizes the founding moment as pure generative act, suppressing the violence and compromise involved. Slavery is constitutive of the founding; the birth metaphor renders it invisible.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: The nation 'conceived in Liberty' was conceived by men who enslaved approximately 700,000 people. The birth metaphor structures the founding as a pure creative act and structurally cannot accommodate enslaved people as co-participants in the birth.

instances.span_text: dedicated to the proposition that all men are created equal

cmt.source_domain: logical_proposition
cmt.target_domain: founding_commitment_to_equality
entailments: equality_is_a_proposition_not_yet_proven
the_nation_exists_to_demonstrate_the_proposition
dedication_to_a_proposition_is_a_logical_commitment
a_proposition_can_be_refuted_by_evidence
war_can_serve_as_a_test_of_the_proposition
cmt.annotation_notes: 'Dedicated to the proposition' — using the term 'proposition' (a logical claim awaiting proof) rather than 'truth' or 'principle' is Lincoln's deliberate choice. It activates the experiment/proof frame that will culminate in S02 ('testing') and S10 ('shall not have died in vain' + 'shall not perish'). The dedication is simultaneously a covenant act (cluster_02) and a logical commitment (cluster_03).

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: Dedication to a proposition is total: you cannot be partially dedicated to a logical claim. The choice is already made at founding; the present generation inherits the dedication as obligation.
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The cause of the war (slavery) is displaced onto an abstract logical proposition (equality). This displacement is structurally required by the experiment metaphor: you cannot test the proposition 'all men are created equal' while naming slavery as the thing being fought about.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: The proposition 'all men are created equal' was the founding formula precisely not applied to enslaved people. Framing the war as a test of this proposition makes enslaved people the object of the test (their freedom is what would prove it) without making them agents of the test.

instances.span_text: testing whether that nation, or any nation so conceived and so dedicated, can long endure

cmt.source_domain: scientific_empirical_test
cmt.target_domain: democratic_self_government_under_pressure
entailments: the_war_is_an_empirical_test
national_survival_is_the_dependent_variable
the_proposition_of_democracy_is_being_measured
the_result_will_constitute_evidence_for_or_against_democracy
the_test_is_universal_any_such_nation
cmt.annotation_notes: 'Can long endure' imports cluster_01 (the nation as organism that can endure or fail to endure = survive or die). The testing frame (cluster_03) and the organism frame (cluster_01) are fused: the test's result is whether the organism survives. This co-activation is the structural engine of the address.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The test is being conducted whether the Union chooses it or not. The war is not a choice Lincoln is making; it is happening TO the proposition. This framing removes Lincoln's agency from the war's conduct entirely.
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: The experiment frame suppresses guilt entirely: the war is not caused by anyone, it is a test that is occurring.
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: War-as-test displaces the question of causation. Who started the war, why, over what — these are not questions a test asks. The test frame makes the war's cause (slavery, secession) irrelevant to its meaning (democratic proof).
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Lincoln is absent as agent — he is not conducting the test, the test is happening. The Confederacy is absent as agent — they are not attacking the Union, they are providing the pressure of the test. Enslaved people are absent — they are the subject of the proposition being tested, not participants in the test.

instances.span_text: so conceived and so dedicated

cmt.target_domain: national_founding_and_purpose

the_birth_origin_defines_what_is_being_tested
cmt.annotation_notes: The echo of S01's 'conceived' and 'dedicated' three sentences later is deliberate. Lincoln's repetition ties the testing frame (cluster_03) back to the birth frame (cluster_04): what is being tested is the infant nation, the born thing. This is the pivot between the founding paragraph and the war paragraph.

instances.span_text: gave their lives that that nation might live

cmt.source_domain: organic_life_and_death
cmt.target_domain: national_survival_through_sacrifice

cmt.annotation_notes: The exchange logic — individual lives for national life — is the first explicit statement of the sacrificial economy. The nation lives because soldiers died. The purposive 'that' clause ('gave their lives THAT the nation might live') makes the exchange explicit and intentional, converting death into a transaction.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The soldiers chose to give their lives; the frame honors the voluntariness of the sacrifice rather than imposing obligation.
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: No guilt assigned; the framing is the honor of sacrifice, not the cause or blame.
koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: national_life
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The purposive 'that' clause converts grief into economy: the deaths were not waste (manic defense against the sheer horror of battlefield death) because they produced something — national survival.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Individual deaths are converted into a single economic transaction ('gave their lives that the nation might live') — the named dead, their particular wounds, their particular bodies, are abstracted away. Black soldiers who were among those dying for Union and emancipation by November 1863 are absent from 'those who here gave their lives.' Enslaved people whose freedom is what the nation's life now formally includes (post-Emancipation Proclamation) are absent as beneficiaries.

instances.span_text: we can not dedicate—we can not consecrate—we can not hallow—this ground

cmt.source_domain: priestly_consecration
cmt.target_domain: human_capacity_to_honor_sacrifice
entailments: sacred_ground_requires_priestly_speech_acts
human_speakers_normally_possess_this_power
the_claim_here_is_that_soldiers_sacrifice_has_already_performed_consecration

cmt.annotation_notes: The three-part anaphora ('we can not dedicate / consecrate / hallow') is itself a speech act — a ritual refusal that performs its own form of consecration. The argument is that soldiers' deaths have done what human speech cannot: made the ground sacred. This is a theological claim (cluster_06) and a covenant-renunciation claim (cluster_02): the ceremony of dedication Lincoln has been asked to perform is declared insufficient before he performs it.

koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: sacred_ground
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The claim that human ceremony is insufficient is a form of reparation: Lincoln is not claiming to honor the dead adequately (manic defense) but acknowledging the limits of language in the face of sacrifice. Paradoxically, this acknowledgment is the address's most powerful rhetorical move.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Lincoln positions himself (and all speakers) as categorically incapable of the act that has already been performed by soldier deaths. This is the most explicit lincoln_non_agent moment in the address: the speaker's role is formally collapsed. Death is abstracted from bodies to an act of consecration.

instances.span_text: have consecrated it, far above our poor power to add or detract

cmt.source_domain: priestly_consecration
cmt.target_domain: soldier_sacrifice_as_sanctifying_act
entailments: soldier_struggle_performs_the_function_of_priestly_consecration
death_in_battle_is_a_sacred_act_not_a_secular_one
human_speech_is_ontologically_inferior_to_soldier_action
the_ground_is_already_sacred_before_any_ceremony
cmt.annotation_notes: The completed action ('have consecrated') places the sanctifying act in the past — it is done, by the soldiers, before Lincoln speaks. This retroactive consecration by death is the theological heart of the address: the soldiers have acted as priests, and Lincoln as civilian orator can only receive and transmit their act.

koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: sacred_ground
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: Lincoln continues to diminish his own power ('our poor power to add or detract') — this is reparative humility in the face of loss, not manic inflation of sacrifice.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Lincoln_non_agent: 'our poor power to add or detract' explicitly diminishes the speaker's role. Death_abstracted: the soldiers' deaths are converted into the act of consecration — an aesthetic and theological product. Black_soldiers_erased: 'the brave men, living and dead, who struggled here' — Black Union soldiers who fought and died at this stage of the war are structurally present in this description but conventionally invisible; the universal 'brave men' erases their specific historical presence and the specific meaning of Black men dying for the emancipation proposition.

instances.span_text: dedicated here to the unfinished work which they who fought here have thus far so nobly advanced

cmt.target_domain: obligation_to_complete_soldiers_work

cmt.annotation_notes: 'Unfinished work' positions the war as an ongoing project with an incomplete deliverable. The dedication is an oath: it is for US (mandatory assignment) to be dedicated (bound by vow). This is the moment where the sacrificial economy converts to obligation: soldier deaths create a debt that the living must repay by completing the work.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: 'It is for us the living' — the obligation is assigned, not chosen. Lincoln uses 'it is for us... to' rather than 'we choose to' or 'we will.' The obligation flows from the fact of the soldiers' deaths, not from any act of will by the living.
koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: obligation_transferred_to_living
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The reparative structure: soldier deaths produce obligation in the living. The living's action (completing the work) repays the debt created by the deaths. This converts grief into ethical program.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Lincoln_non_agent: 'it is for us the living... to be dedicated' — Lincoln receives the obligation rather than choosing it. Death_abstracted: the soldiers' deaths are converted into the 'work' they have advanced — the deaths become productive acts rather than losses. Enslaved_people_non_agent: the 'unfinished work' is the war's prosecution, with emancipation as one of its elements, but enslaved people are not among 'us the living' who are obligated to complete the work.

instances.span_text: the last full measure of devotion

cmt.target_domain: soldiers_total_self_sacrifice

'Last full measure' is the total depletion of a resource — the soldiers gave everything. This sets up the implicit obligation on the living to give 'increased devotion,' which is a lesser quantity but in the same economy.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: If the soldiers gave the last full measure, the living's devotion — which is measured against that total — is implicitly demanded. You cannot receive total sacrifice and respond with partial commitment.
koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: devotion_transferred_to_living
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: Converting death into 'the last full measure of devotion' is manic defense against the reality of bodily destruction: soldiers did not give a 'measure' of something, they were killed. The economic-quantitative metaphor transforms slaughter into gift.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Death_abstracted: individual deaths become 'the last full measure of devotion' — an abstract economic unit. Black_soldiers_erased: present among the honored dead but not distinguished; their sacrifice carried additional weight (proof that Black men would fight and die for freedom) that is erased by universalization.

instances.span_text: these dead shall not have died in vain

cmt.source_domain: logical_proof_and_evidence
cmt.target_domain: soldier_deaths_as_evidence_for_democracy

cmt.annotation_notes: 'Died in vain' is the experiment's failure condition: if democracy collapses, the deaths were waste, not proof. The resolve ('we here highly resolve') is the act that will prevent logical failure — it is the living's commitment to supply the conclusion that the deaths were meaningful. This converts a vow into a logical operation.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The resolve is mandatory: if the deaths would otherwise be 'vain' (wasted, null), then the living must ensure they are not. The logical form of the obligation is: premises (deaths) require a valid conclusion (the nation endures); the living must supply that conclusion.
koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: propositional_proof
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The resolve that deaths shall not be 'in vain' is the purest expression of the manic defense in the address: it converts the terror that the deaths MIGHT be vain (purposeless, wasteful) into a determination to make them meaningful. The horror is acknowledged in the subjunctive ('shall not have died in vain') and immediately converted into resolve.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Death_abstracted: individual deaths are converted into logical units in an argument — they are premisses, not losses. Enslaved_people_non_agent: the proposition being proven ('that all men are created equal') concerns their status, but they are absent from the argument's dramaturgy. Black_soldiers_erased: present in the 'honored dead' but erased as a specific population whose deaths carry a specific and additional meaning.

instances.span_text: a new birth of freedom

cmt.source_domain: birth_generation
cmt.target_domain: post_war_freedom_and_national_renewal
entailments: freedom_is_born_not_restored

cmt.annotation_notes: This is the address's climactic metaphor and among the most important in the corpus. 'New birth' closes the arc that 'brought forth... conceived' opened in S01. The nation was born in 1776; it is being reborn through war. The word 'new' is critical: this is not restoration (cluster_01's wound logic) but genuine generation (cluster_04). The birth resolves the test (cluster_03): democracy is not just surviving, it is reproducing. The three clusters co-activate simultaneously in four words.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: If this birth is what the war produces, then the war must be won for the birth to occur. The birth logic makes victory obligatory — you cannot abandon the birth.
koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: new_birth_of_freedom
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The conversion of mass death into birth is the ultimate manic defense: the war that kills thousands is the labor that produces freedom. The horror of the killing is absorbed into the metaphor of nativity, which is culturally coded as hope, not horror.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Enslaved_people_non_agent: the 'new birth of freedom' is most directly about emancipation — it is enslaved people's freedom being born. But they are not named, not present, not agents of their own birth. Their freedom is something born for them and through soldiers' deaths. Black_soldiers_erased: 180,000 Black Union soldiers are among those whose deaths are the labor of this birth; they are absent as a named group. Death_abstracted: deaths become birth-labor, converted from loss into generative act. Women_absent: birth metaphors conventionally erase women as the actual laborers of birth — the birth here happens without mothers.

instances.span_text: shall not perish from the earth

cmt.source_domain: organic_death_and_survival
cmt.target_domain: democratic_government_persistence
entailments: democratic_government_is_a_living_organism

cmt.annotation_notes: The address closes with an organism metaphor — government that might 'perish from the earth.' This echoes and resolves the 'endure' of S02: the organism that was under test (S02, 'can long endure') is now affirmed in its survival. 'Perish from the earth' is extinction language — the species (democratic self-government) must not go extinct. This is cluster_01 at maximum extension: the organism is now not the Union but the entire project of democratic governance, globally.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: If democratic government perishing would be a catastrophe equivalent to extinction, then preventing it is an absolute obligation — there is no price too high to pay for survival. The extinction frame maximizes the obligatory logic.
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The scale of the projected entity expands to 'government of the people, by the people, for the people' — not the Union specifically, not the question of slavery, but all democratic self-government everywhere. This displacement converts a civil war about slavery into a defense of a universal principle, which is both Lincoln's genuine belief and a displacement of the specific cause.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Enslaved_people_non_agent: government 'of the people, by the people, for the people' — enslaved people are not among 'the people' with political standing in this formulation; the universal claim of the address does not extend to them as political agents. Confederates_depersonalized: the threat to democratic government is abstract (it might perish) rather than the Confederacy specifically.

cmt.annotation_notes: This sentence is the earliest full draft of the Second Inaugural's theodicy. Comparing: Hodges Letter (April 1864) says 'God now wills the removal of a great wrong...pay fairly for our complicity'; Second Inaugural (March 1865) says 'He now wills to remove [slavery]...every drop of blood drawn with the lash shall be paid by another drawn with the sword.' The Second Inaugural adds the lash-sword specificity (naming slavery's physical violence for the first time) and the cosmic guilt frame (both sides as God's willed participants). But the theological structure is already complete here. Note the absence flag: enslaved people are 'the great wrong' — not the wronged, but the wrong itself. They are objectified as the moral problem God is solving, not as persons whose suffering created the debt. Moved legacy sacrificial_economy prose into sacrificial_yield.

mapping.God wills: active divine causation of historical events
mapping.removal of a great wrong: God as surgeon removing the national pathogen — but note: removal, not purification; the distinction from disease_and_purification logic holds
mapping.pay fairly for our complicity: debt-payment economy — guilt creates obligation; the war is the payment mechanism
mapping.North as well as South: distributed guilt — both sections are complicit

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: God's will is not negotiable. If God wills the removal and wills payment, the war must continue until the account is settled — Lincoln's will is irrelevant.
koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: The payment frame: guilt (complicity in slavery) creates a debt; the war is the currency of payment; God administers the exchange. This is the direct precursor to the Second Inaugural's 'every drop of blood drawn with the lash / paid by another drawn with the sword' (inst_00024).
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Legacy annotation flagged structural absence without a separate absence_notes field; see annotation_notes and migration notes.

Confidence adjusted down to 0.77 (0.82 base − 0.05 campaign_speech register).

mapping.great emergencies: the experimental conditions — extreme stress that will determine whether the proposition holds

instances.span_text: brought our republic to a severe test

cmt.source_domain: scientific testing — a controlled trial that stresses a subject to determine its limits
cmt.target_domain: the Civil War as the experimental condition testing whether democratic self-government can survive
entailments: The republic is under controlled stress — the experiment is happening now
The strain is quantifiable — the election added measurable additional stress
A 'severe test' implies expected difficulty before proof is established
cmt.annotation_notes: The campaign_speech register makes this cluster_03 activation particularly significant: Lincoln is using the experiment frame with a celebratory audience, not in a formal address. This suggests cluster_03 is not reserved for high-rhetoric occasions — it is Lincoln's default epistemic frame for the war's purpose.

mapping.republic: the experimental subject — the proposition made institutional
mapping.severe test: the experimental trial — maximum stress conditions
mapping.added not a little to the strain: the wartime election increased the experimental load

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The republic must survive the severe test to demonstrate the proposition. Failing the test — allowing the election to be cancelled — would disprove the proposition and effectively end the experiment. This makes surviving the test obligatory.

instances.span_text: demonstrated that a people's government can sustain a national election

cmt.source_domain: scientific demonstration — empirical evidence proving a proposition
cmt.target_domain: the election result as proof that democratic government can survive wartime
entailments: The election result is data, not just politics — it proves a previously unproven proposition
The demonstration is valid because conditions were maximally stressful
Until this demonstration, the proposition was unproven — 'Until now it has not been known to the world'
cmt.annotation_notes: This sentence completes the ext_010 arc: hypothesis (p02) → test (p03) → demonstration (p06). The word 'demonstrated' is precise: not 'showed,' not 'suggested,' but 'demonstrated' — the register of scientific proof. This is cluster_03 at its most explicit and unguarded. The subsequent sentence ('Until now it has not been known to the world that this was a possibility') confirms that the demonstration has global epistemic significance — it is not just an American election but a proof for the world.

mapping.demonstrated: empirical proof — the election produced evidence that resolves the prior question
mapping.a people's government can sustain: the proposition is confirmed — the experimental subject survived the trial
mapping.in the midst of a great civil war: the testing conditions — maximum stress — under which the demonstration was valid

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The demonstration closes the experiment: the proposition is now proven. But the war that created the experimental conditions must continue until the proof is complete (the rebellion crushed). The evidentiary logic requires seeing the experiment through.

instances.span_text: how sound, and how strong we still are

cmt.target_domain: the republic's continued vitality after surviving the wartime election

The 'still' implies a feared alternative: the body could have been weakened
cmt.annotation_notes: 'Sound' is the key word: in medical usage, a 'sound' body is one free of internal disease or structural damage. The republic being 'sound' after the election stress is the cluster_01 health-check that complements the cluster_03 experimental proof. The two clusters work together: the experiment proved the proposition (cluster_03); the health check confirmed no damage was sustained (cluster_01).

mapping.sound: the medical/structural assessment — no internal damage, no disease
mapping.strong: physical vitality — the organism's capacity to resist further stress
mapping.still are: the adverb of survival — still sound despite what was feared

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The health-check confirms the organism is intact. If the body is still sound, it can continue fighting — the war's continuation is medically authorized by the republic's resilience.

In the campaign_speech register, this framing also functions to defuse post-election bitterness: if God chose Lincoln, opposing him is opposing God.

instances.span_text: dissolve the Union

instances.span_text: let it perish

cmt.source_domain: organic_death
cmt.target_domain: collapse_of_the_Union
entailments: the_nation_can_die_not_merely_fail
perishing_is_absolute_irreversible_extinction
preventing_perishing_is_an_absolute_imperative
the_Union_is_alive_and_mortal
cmt.annotation_notes: The echo of Gettysburg's 'shall not perish from the earth' is deliberate. Lincoln is completing the arc: at Gettysburg the resolve was that government shall not perish; here he describes the frame in which the Union side chose war rather than allow that perishing. The extinction metaphor generates the strongest possible obligatory frame.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: Same frame as the paired survive instance — the organism cannot be allowed to perish; this makes war the only option.
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: The sentence distributes action across both parties: 'one of them would make war... the other would accept war.' Neither side is solely guilty; both faced the same terminal choice.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Lincoln is again absent as agent — the two parties made choices; Lincoln is not among them in this framing.

instances.span_text: the war came

cmt.source_domain: natural_event_arrival
cmt.target_domain: outbreak_of_civil_war
entailments: the_war_is_an_entity_that_moves_and_arrives
it_came_from_somewhere_outside_human_will
no_human_agent_caused_it_to_come
arrival_events_are_not_chosen_by_those_to_whom_they_arrive
cmt.annotation_notes: Three words that perform the most radical removal of human agency in Lincoln's corpus. After describing how both parties deprecated war, one would make war rather than allow secession, the other would accept war rather than allow dissolution — Lincoln could have written 'and war broke out' or 'and the fighting began.' Instead: 'and the war came.' The war is an entity with autonomous motion. No one chose it; it arrived. This is the pivot on which the entire address's guilt distribution turns: if the war came (was not caused by human will), then the question of blame is replaced by the question of meaning. The co-activation of cluster_06 is latent here and will be explicit in S22: if the war came, then it was sent — and by whom?

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: A war that 'comes' rather than being started cannot be resisted or negotiated with in the same way as a war with a human aggressor. Its obligatory character is cosmological: you do not debate whether to accept a storm.
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: The agentless construction is the pivot to cosmic guilt distribution. The war has no human cause that can be assigned as guilt. It came — implying it was sent. By what or whom will be answered in S22.
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The agency of both parties (one made war, the other accepted war) is displaced onto an intransitive arrival event. This displacement is not evasion but a genuine theological claim: the war's ultimate cause is not human will.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Lincoln_non_agent: Lincoln is nowhere in this sentence. Confederates_depersonalized: the Confederate choice to make war is dissolved into the war's autonomous arrival. Enslaved_people_non_agent: slavery is the cause of the war; enslaved people's condition is what the war is about; but 'the war came' removes all agents from the moment of causation.

instances.span_text: rend the Union

cmt.source_domain: tearing_flesh
cmt.target_domain: Confederate_destruction_of_the_Union
entailments: the_Union_is_a_body_or_fabric_with_physical_integrity
rending_is_violent_tearing_of_connected_material
the_torn_thing_bleeds_or_suffers
rending_is_irreparable_without_binding
cmt.annotation_notes: 'Rend' is a precise and violent verb — to tear apart, especially flesh or cloth. In biblical usage, rending garments is a sign of grief or outrage. Lincoln's choice of 'rend' over 'break' or 'split' or 'divide' loads the Confederate act with physical violence against a living body. The wound that must be 'bound up' in S25 is the wound created by this rending in S14.

koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: One of the clearest external guilt-distribution sentences in the address — the insurgents are the agents who would rend. This is the wound-creation event that the address will then transcend through cosmic guilt.

instances.span_text: The Almighty has His own purposes

cmt.source_domain: divine_intentionality
cmt.target_domain: the_wars_ultimate_cause_and_meaning
entailments: God_is_an_agent_with_intentions
those_intentions_are_not_identical_to_human_intentions
human_purposes_are_therefore_insufficient_explanations_of_events
discerning_Gods_purposes_is_necessary_to_understanding_the_war
cmt.annotation_notes: The pivot sentence of the address. Everything before this — both parties, both prayers, both expectations — has been shown to be insufficient. Now Lincoln introduces the only adequate explanatory agent: the Almighty's own purposes. The sentence is short, declarative, and unanswerable. Assigning ext_005 to the theodicy arc of the Second Inaugural's fourth paragraph.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: If the Almighty has His own purposes, then the war is obligatory in a cosmic sense: it cannot be stopped by human will since it was not started by human will. Ending the war requires God's will, not human negotiation.
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: The ultimate guilt distribution: neither party is adequately explained as cause; God's purposes are the real causal agent. This is the culmination of the drift from external → distributed → cosmic across Lincoln's diachronic arc.
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: Human moral agency — including the Confederate choice to secede and Lincoln's choice to fight — is displaced onto divine intentionality. This is not evasion in Lincoln's framework but a genuine theological position: the war is larger than any human purpose.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Lincoln_non_agent: Lincoln's own agency in the war is subsumed into the Almighty's purposes — he is not a chooser but an instrument. Enslaved_people_non_agent: slavery is constructed as God's 'offense' — a category of divine-human relation — rather than as a system maintained by human choices that victimized specific human beings.

instances.span_text: in the providence of God, must needs come, but which, having continued through His appointed time, He now wills to remove

cmt.source_domain: divine_temporal_administration
cmt.target_domain: slavery_as_providential_offense_with_appointed_end
entailments: God_administers_historical_time
slavery_had_a_divinely_appointed_duration
God_now_wills_its_removal
the_war_is_the_instrument_of_that_removal
human_abolition_efforts_are_secondary_to_divine_will
cmt.annotation_notes: The most theologically specific claim in Lincoln's public rhetoric: slavery was divinely permitted for an appointed time, and God now wills its removal. This claim makes abolition a providential event rather than a moral or political choice. The entailment that makes this analytically critical: if God wills slavery's removal, then human abolitionists (Black and white), enslaved people's resistance, and Lincoln's own actions are all subordinate instruments of divine will — none of them the primary agent.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: God's will to remove slavery through war makes the war obligatory at the cosmic level. Lincoln cannot stop a war God is prosecuting.
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: Slavery's existence and removal are both attributed to God's will — this distributes any guilt for slavery's perpetuation to the cosmic level ('must needs come') rather than to specific human actors.
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The cause of the war (human slavery, human secession) is displaced onto divine temporal administration. This displacement is Lincoln's most consistent and most analytically significant psychic move.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Enslaved_people_non_agent: their two hundred and fifty years of suffering and resistance is here administered by God's appointed time — they are not agents of their own liberation but subjects of divine scheduling. Lincoln_non_agent: Lincoln's Emancipation Proclamation is subsumed into 'He now wills to remove.'

instances.span_text: He gives to both North and South this terrible war as the woe due to those by whom the offense came

cmt.source_domain: divine_punishment_distribution
cmt.target_domain: the_war_as_Gods_judgment_on_both_sides
entailments: God_gives_the_war_as_a_gift_of_punishment
both_sides_receive_the_punishment
the_punishment_is_proportionate_to_the_offense
the_offense_is_slavery
both_sides_participated_in_the_offense
cmt.annotation_notes: The culmination of the guilt_distribution arc across Lincoln's entire corpus: God gives the war to BOTH North and South. The North is not exempt from culpability. 'By whom the offense came' applies to both sections — the North participated in slavery through the constitutional compact, through commerce, through enforcement of the Fugitive Slave Act. This is distributed guilt elevated to cosmic distribution: God is the distributor, both parties are the recipients.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: A war given by God as due punishment cannot be stopped by human negotiation — it must run its course until the divine purpose is satisfied.
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: The most explicit distributed guilt statement in the corpus, and the most theologically grounded: both parties receive the punishment because both participated in the offense. This is the Second Inaugural's central moral claim.
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The reparative structure: by accepting shared guilt, Lincoln gestures toward a post-war reconciliation that does not require the South's permanent humiliation. This is the theological foundation of 'malice toward none' — if both sides earned the punishment, neither side can be uniquely condemned.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Enslaved people are the victims of the offense (slavery) but they are not among 'those by whom the offense came' — they did not bring the offense. Yet they are also absent as a distinct category of those who suffered the war's punishment. They gave the most (their labor, their blood under the lash) and are absent from the divine accounting.

instances.span_text: this mighty scourge of war

cmt.source_domain: divine_instrument_of_punishment
cmt.target_domain: the_civil_war
entailments: the_war_is_a_whip_wielded_by_a_divine_hand
the_scourge_is_applied_to_punish_not_to_purify
the_scourge_passes_when_the_punishment_is_complete
humans_can_pray_for_its_passing_but_not_command_it
cmt.annotation_notes: CRITICAL: 'Scourge' is the closest Lincoln comes to disease_and_purification language in the corpus. A scourge can mean a whip (divine punishment instrument) or a plague/pestilence. The distinction matters enormously: if scourge = divine whip (punishment), the violence logic is punitive/restorative (cluster_06, fantasy_type=punishment_and_theodicy); if scourge = plague (purification), it would approach disease_and_purification logic. The context (paired with 'God wills that it continue' and 'the judgments of the Lord are righteous') firmly establishes this as divine-punishment logic, not disease-purification logic. The war is the whip in God's hand, not a pathogen to be expelled. This distinction is the key structural finding: Lincoln never constructs the war as a purifying agent that removes a contaminating social group. The Confederacy is not a pathogen; it is a party that also deserves punishment. The exit condition (scourge passes when punishment is complete) is confirmed.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The scourge continues until God wills otherwise — it cannot be put down by human decision. Lincoln and his audience are under the scourge, not wielding it.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Lincoln prays that the scourge may pass — he is a supplicant under it, not the one wielding it. This is the most explicit lincoln_non_agent moment in the address.

instances.span_text: every drop of blood drawn with the lash shall be paid by another drawn with the sword

cmt.source_domain: blood_as_economic_currency
cmt.target_domain: the_providential_equivalence_of_slavery_violence_and_war_violence
entailments: blood_is_a_quantifiable_substance_that_can_be_drawn_and_paid
a_debt_in_blood_requires_repayment_in_blood
the_lash_drew_blood_from_enslaved_people
the_sword_draws_blood_from_soldiers
the_economy_is_divinely_administered
the_debt_must_be_paid_in_full_before_the_war_ends
cmt.annotation_notes: The most analytically significant sentence in the Second Inaugural and one of the most important in the corpus. The blood economy operates as follows: (1) the lash draws blood from enslaved bodies — this is the debt; (2) the sword draws blood from soldiers' bodies — this is the payment. The payment is structurally commensurate: drop for drop, lash for sword. God administers the exchange. The implications are extraordinary: Lincoln is saying that soldiers' deaths in the war are the precise equivalent of enslaved people's suffering under slavery. This is the closest Lincoln comes to explicitly making the enslaved people's bodily suffering the moral cause of the war. But the framing is also the most problematic: enslaved people are the creditors of this debt (their blood was drawn first) but they are absent as agents in the repayment. Soldiers pay; enslaved people were paid upon. The blood economy converts their suffering into the material of a divine accounting that does not name them as its beneficiaries.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The debt must be paid in full — 'every drop... shall be paid.' This is a total obligation with no discretionary element. The war cannot end until the debt is settled.
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: Both slaveholders (whose lash drew blood) and the nation that tolerated them (whose sons now pay in sword-blood) bear the debt. The distribution is not equal — slavery's perpetrators drew blood first — but both parties pay.
koenigsberg.sacrificial_yield: debt_payment_enabling_war_end
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The blood economy is a reparative structure at the cosmic level: the debt created by slavery is being repaid through soldier deaths. The reparation is not to enslaved people (they do not receive the payment) but to divine justice.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Enslaved_people_non_agent: the entire blood economy is about their suffering (lash drawing blood) but they are not named, not present as agents, not identified as the creditors whose debt is being repaid. Their blood is the premise; soldiers' blood is the conclusion; enslaved people themselves are absent from the transaction. Black_soldiers_erased: Black Union soldiers' blood is drawn with the sword in payment of the debt — they are doubly erased: as enslaved people (who paid first) and as soldiers (who now pay again). Death_abstracted: soldier deaths are converted into blood-currency in a divine payment scheme.

instances.span_text: finish the work we are in

cmt.source_domain: votive_labor_completion
cmt.target_domain: obligation_to_conclude_the_war_and_its_purposes
entailments: the_war_is_a_work_in_progress
works_have_completion_conditions
those_engaged_in_the_work_are_obligated_to_finish_it
leaving_work_unfinished_dishonors_those_who_began_it
cmt.annotation_notes: Echoes Gettysburg's 'the unfinished work which they who fought here have thus far so nobly advanced.' The same labor frame: the war is a work with an incomplete state that requires completion. The oath/obligation frame (cluster_02) is primary — 'let us strive on' is a votive commitment. The birth-labor frame (cluster_04) is co-activated: finishing the work = completing the labor that will produce the new birth.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: 'Let us strive on to finish' — the obligation to complete is absolute. The work cannot be abandoned mid-process.
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: 'We are in' — the first person plural includes Lincoln and his audience in the obligation equally.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: The 'work we are in' is the war and its purposes, which include emancipation. Enslaved people are the primary beneficiaries of this work but are not among 'us' who are striving to finish it.

instances.span_text: bind up the nation's wounds

cmt.source_domain: wound_dressing_and_healing
cmt.target_domain: national_reconciliation_after_war
entailments: the_nation_has_wounds_from_the_war
wounds_require_binding_to_begin_healing
the_binder_is_physician_to_the_national_body
healing_is_possible_but_requires_active_care
binding_is_restorative_not_punitive
cmt.annotation_notes: The canonical cluster_01 instance — arguably the most important wound-healing metaphor in the corpus and in American political rhetoric. Placed at the climax of an address that has just described the war as God's punishment, it pivots from cosmic/punitive logic back to restorative logic. After distributing guilt cosmically (both sides earned the scourge), Lincoln calls for restorative action (bind the wounds). The sequence is theologically coherent: punishment completes → wounds can be bound. This is the structural difference from disease_and_purification: wounds have a binding phase; disease requires expulsion of the pathogen. The absence of any expulsion logic in this sentence is the key finding: the physician binds the wound of both sides, not the wound of one side. 'Malice toward none' is the clinical stance of a physician who treats all patients regardless of how they were wounded.

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The wound exists; the wound requires binding; therefore binding is obligatory. The wound logic generates the same necessity as the sword logic — you do not leave wounds unbound.
koenigsberg.guilt_distribution_notes: Binding up 'the nation's wounds' — not the South's wounds — distributes the healing requirement across the whole nation. Both sides are wounded; both need binding.
koenigsberg.psychic_defense_notes: The physician-nation relationship is a reparative structure: guilt acknowledged (distributed/cosmic), now converted into restorative action. This is the address's resolution of its own theological argument.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Enslaved_people_non_agent: the nation's wounds include slavery's wound to enslaved people — but they are absent as patients who need binding. The wounds to be bound are the war's wounds to the national body, not slavery's wounds to Black bodies. Black_soldiers_erased: those who fought and died, including 180,000 Black soldiers, are represented by 'him who shall have borne the battle and for his widow and his orphan' — an abstract figure. The Black soldier's widow and orphan are invisible.

Gratitude is owed to the divine source before it is owed to human actors
cmt.annotation_notes: lincoln_non_agent: Lincoln explicitly disclaims credit for military victory (p01_s07: 'no part of the honor, for plan or execution, is mine'). The cluster_06 opening reinforces this — God is the source of victory, Grant and the army are the instruments, Lincoln is the transmitter of good news. The final address opens with the most complete subordination of human agency to divine source in the corpus. Migrated legacy fantasy_type 'divine_sanction' to canonical 'punishment_and_theodicy'.

mapping.He from whom all blessings flow: God as the origin-point of all good — victory and peace are blessings that flow from God, not achievements of human effort alone
mapping.must not be forgotten: the human tendency is to credit military commanders; Lincoln redirects to the divine source

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: Victory flows from God — the Union's military success is a divine blessing, not a human accomplishment. The cluster_06 frame appears in the opening sentence of the last address, ensuring the entire subsequent political discussion (Reconstruction) occurs within a providential frame.
koenigsberg.absence_notes: Legacy annotation flagged structural absence without a separate absence_notes field; see annotation_notes and migration notes.

instances.span_text: out of their proper relation with the Union...into that proper practical relation

cmt.source_domain: a covenant or relational bond that defines proper standing — states have a proper relation to the Union that can be disrupted and restored
cmt.target_domain: the political-constitutional relationship between seceded states and the federal Union
entailments: States have a proper relation to the Union — a normative position defined by the covenant
Secession is a breach of that proper relation, not an exit from it
Reconstruction is restoration to proper relation, not creation of a new relation
cmt.annotation_notes: The 'proper relation' formula appears here for the third time (after doc_013 p06_s01 and doc_015 p05 quoted passage). In the Proclamation context it was 'restoring the constitutional relation'; here it is 'proper practical relation.' The shift from 'constitutional' to 'practical' reflects Lincoln's deliberate sidestepping of the constitutional question (in or out of the Union) — he argues the practical relation is what matters, regardless of the constitutional answer.

mapping.proper relation: the covenant-defined standing of states within the Union — a relational position with normative force
mapping.out of their proper relation: breach of the covenant position — dislocation from the covenanted relationship
mapping.get them into that proper practical rela: restoration — returning to the covenanted standing

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The 'proper relation' formula carries the same restorative logic as the Emancipation Proclamations' 'restoring the constitutional relation.' Here it is applied to the postwar political question: Reconstruction is not building something new but restoring what was proper. The covenant frame makes the end-state already defined — proper relation — rather than open to political invention.

instances.span_text: feed it, and grow it, and ripen it to a complete success

cmt.source_domain: agricultural cultivation — feeding, growing, and ripening a plant to full maturity
cmt.target_domain: the Louisiana government as a living thing that must be nurtured to its full realized form
entailments: The Louisiana government is a living thing in early development, not a finished structure
Nurture (feeding, growing) is the right relationship to an immature living thing
Ripeness is achievable — the government can reach full success if properly sustained
cmt.annotation_notes: Part of ext_011 (cultivation/birth arc): inst_00063 establishes the cultivation frame; inst_00064 makes it explicit with the egg-to-fowl metaphor. The accumulation of verbs ('feed...grow...ripen') builds the agricultural analogy progressively before the egg metaphor names it directly. Migrated legacy fantasy_type 'generative_creation' to canonical 'birth_and_creation'.

mapping.feed it: provide the resources needed for the government's growth — as a plant needs water and nutrients
mapping.grow it: allow the government to develop its full form — as a plant grows toward maturity
mapping.ripen it to a complete success: the government reaching its mature realized form — ripeness as the moment of full actualization

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The Reconstruction government must be nurtured rather than abandoned — the agricultural/birth frame makes rejection (smashing, dispersing) an act of violence against a living thing. The obligation to support Louisiana flows from the fact that it is alive and growing.

instances.span_text: as the egg is to the fowl, we shall sooner have the fowl by hatching the egg than by smashing it

cmt.source_domain: egg hatching — a living thing in developmental incubation that must be brought to birth through patience, not destroyed
cmt.target_domain: the Louisiana Reconstruction government as an immature form that will become its full self if allowed to develop
entailments: The Louisiana government is alive in its current form, even if imperfect
Patience and support (hatching) will produce the desired full form
Rejection (smashing) destroys not just the current form but all future possibility
The hatched fowl is already implicit in the egg — the final form is the current form's natural destination
cmt.annotation_notes: Marked novel: the egg-to-fowl ratio is a specific and original analogical form. Lincoln is not using a conventional egg metaphor but constructing a precise structural analogy: current government : ideal government :: egg : fowl. The conditional structure ('Concede that...as the egg is to the fowl') makes the analogy explicit and self-aware — Lincoln acknowledges the gap between current and ideal while using the developmental frame to argue for patience over rejection. Migrated legacy fantasy_type 'generative_creation' to canonical 'birth_and_creation'.

mapping.the egg: the Louisiana government in its current immature form — alive but not yet fully realized
mapping.the fowl: the mature realized Louisiana government — what it will become
mapping.hatching the egg: the patient process of supporting development until the government reaches its full form
mapping.smashing it: destroying the egg before it can hatch — rejecting the Louisiana government destroys the possibility of the mature form

koenigsberg.obligatory_frame_notes: The egg-to-fowl metaphor reframes the Reconstruction debate: the question is not whether the Louisiana government is good enough (the egg is not the fowl) but whether it can become what it should be (the egg can hatch). Smashing the egg — rejecting Louisiana — is an act of violence against a nascent living thing. The birth frame makes nurture the moral default and rejection a form of destruction. This is the inverse of the war's violence logic: in reconstruction, violence (smashing) destroys what nonviolence (hatching) creates.
